evidence: The three-line structure was not designed for AI agents. title_or_actor: Jieke Pan
evidence: Who owns AI in a private bank?  Wrong question.  - The CIO wants the platform. - The COO wants the workflow. - The CDO wants the data. - The CRO wants the thresholds. - Compliance wants the guardrails. - The Head of Wealth wants the RM experience.  They are all right, and that is exactly why most AI programs stall.  Agentic AI does not belong to a single function. It cuts across technology, operations, governance, risk, compliance, and client engagement simultaneously.  The real question is: Who owns which AI decisions? Because ownership of: the agent architecture, the workflow, the knowledge layer, the autonomy thresholds, and the RM experience… they cannot sit in one department anymore.  The banks making real progress with AI are not starting with copilots or models. They are starting with decision rights. In private banking, a badly governed AI system is not just a technology problem.
